title: A floor phase's on-disk deliverable verdict (audit-report evolve-verdict PASS) is overridden by the bridge SESSION-LIFECYCLE verdict (audit-usage.json FAIL) that gets stamped when the NEXT phase context-cancels the audit session — discards good audited cycles (2 of 4 width-2 storm failures)
summary: RETRO-DIAGNOSED live 2026-07-14, cycles 824 AND 825 (both index-lock/merge-rung0 lanes), verbatim [D1]: 'phase verdict resolved FAIL despite BOTH authoritative verdict surfaces = PASS.' On-disk audit-report.md evolve-verdict = PASS (0.95) and routing-decision = PASS, every gate green (TDD RED->green, go test -race 4/4, coverage 96.2%>=90%, adversarial PASS, ACS red_count==0 PASS). The ONLY surface that said FAIL: audit-usage.json:verdict — the phase-supervisor's SESSION-LIFECYCLE verdict — stamped at the exact instant the retrospective phase started and context-CANCELED the audit bridge session (audit-observer-events.ndjson: 'stopped ... reason: context canceled'). The cycle verdict resolver read that session-lifecycle FAIL instead of the on-disk deliverable PASS -> routing-decision = audit-fail-to-retrospective -> cycle discarded, worktree preserved, good audited work thrown away and requeued from scratch. TWO defects compound: (a) PRECEDENCE — a floor phase's on-disk deliverable verdict (the machine-readable evolve-verdict line the auditor wrote) MUST be authoritative over the session-lifecycle/liveness status; a session teardown is telemetry, not a phase verdict. (b) TEARDOWN RACE — the next phase (retro) starting context-cancels the prior phase's (audit) session and back-stamps FAIL AFTER the deliverable+verdict were already written; the audit session must be fully harvested and marked complete BEFORE any next-phase start or context-cancel, and a cancel that lands after harvest is benign cleanup that stamps NO verdict. This is the SAME disease the cycle-802 floor-only-verdict fix targeted (a non-authoritative surface overriding a floor PASS) but on a DIFFERENT surface: final_verdict_floor.go guards the phase-loop FinalVerdict assignment; it does NOT govern which SURFACE a floor phase's verdict is read from. Distinct from signalcenter_exhaustion (that is liveness DETECTION; this is verdict PRECEDENCE + teardown ordering).
acceptance: RED: TestPhaseVerdict_DeliverablePASSBeatsSessionLifecycleFAIL — audit-report evolve-verdict=PASS + audit-usage.json verdict=FAIL(session canceled) => resolved cycle verdict = PASS, ship proceeds
RED: TestAuditSession_HarvestedBeforeNextPhaseStarts — starting retro/next phase does NOT context-cancel the audit session until its deliverable verdict is harvested and the phase marked complete
RED: TestSessionCancelAfterHarvest_StampsNoVerdict — a context-cancel landing after the deliverable is written records cleanup, not a FAIL verdict
width-2 soak: cycles with all-PASS floor deliverables SHIP (no audit-fail-to-retrospective on a PASS deliverable); go test -race PASS; apicover clean
source: cycles 824 + 825 retrospective [D1] 2026-07-14 (audit-usage.json session-lifecycle FAIL vs audit-report.md PASS 0.95); routing-decision-8/11.json = audit-fail-to-retrospective
